hero.title: Moonlit Dining on the Adriatic
hero.description: An intimate fine-dining experience where Mediterranean elegance meets Montenegrin hospitality — perched above the pristine waters of Sveti Stefan.

experience.title: Crafted by the Sea, Served by Moonlight
experience.body: Set within the boutique Villa Geba, Muse draws on the day's Adriatic catch and the orchards of the Montenegrin coast. Each plate is a quiet study in restraint and craft — paired with refined wines and the slow rhythm of a coastal evening.

values.d: Every plate begins with the day's Adriatic catch and the orchards of the Montenegrin shore.
No spectacle — only craft, restraint, and the slow rhythm of a coastal evening.
Dining woven into Villa Geba and the moonlit waters of Sveti Stefan.

items.name: Adriatic Crudo
items.desc: Day-boat sea bass, citrus, Montenegrin olive oil

items.name: Octopus & Fava
items.desc: Slow-cooked octopus, broad-bean purée, smoked paprika

items.name: Burrata della Costa
items.desc: Stracciatella, fig, toasted pine, aged balsamic

items.name: Lamb Under the Bell
items.desc: Montenegrin peka, root vegetables, rosemary jus

reviews.name: Elena Marković
reviews.testimonial: An unforgettable evening — the sea bass was flawless and the terrace at dusk is pure magic. Service that anticipates every need.

reviews.name: James Whitfield
reviews.testimonial: The most refined dining on the Montenegrin coast. Quiet luxury, exquisite Adriatic flavours, and a wine list to linger over.

reviews.name: Sofia Conti
reviews.testimonial: Muse turns dinner into a slow, candlelit ritual. The black risotto and the moonlit views keep drawing us back.

reviews.name: Marko Petrović
reviews.testimonial: Peka lamb under the bell, done the proper way. This is how the coast should taste — and the room is beautiful.

reviews.name: Amelie Rousseau
reviews.testimonial: We came for our anniversary and left speechless. Every detail, from the lighting to the langoustines, was perfect.

reviews.name: Daniel Berg
reviews.testimonial: A genuinely thoughtful Montenegrin and Italian list, poured by people who know it. The octopus starter alone is worth the trip.
